name: sftkit
documentation:
# SFTKit

A general purpose collection of base building blocks and utilities to make building 
python applications on the basis of postgresql (asyncpg) + fastapi a breeze.

To get started simply run

```bash
pip install sftkit
```

## Usage


### Dev CLI

Configure sftkit in your `pyproject.toml`

```toml
[tool.sftkit]
db_code_dir = "<path-to-your-sql-code-folder>"
db_migrations_dir = "<path-to-your-sql-data-migrations>"
```

Create new migrations via

```bash
sftkit create-migration <name>
```

connection: open database connection

initial_run: true if we shall call the handler once after startup with None as argument.

Implements a database hook to subscribe to one specific pg_notify notification channel.

functions.name: sftkit.util.log_setup
documentation:
Perform setup for the logger.
Run before any logging.log thingy is called.

if setting is 0: the default is used, which is WARNING.
else: setting + default is used.

functions.name: sftkit.util.clamp
documentation: return number but limit it to the inclusive given value range

functions.name: sftkit.util.run_as_fg_process
documentation:
the "correct" way of spawning a new subprocess:
signals like C-c must only go
to the child process, and not to this python.

the args are the same as subprocess.Popen

returns Popen().wait() value

Some side-info about "how ctrl-c works":
https://unix.stackexchange.com/a/149756/1321

fun fact: this function took a whole night
          to be figured out.

modules.name: sftkit.devel._debian
documentation: !/usr/bin/python3
Build the Debian packages using Docker images.
This script builds the Docker images and then executes them sequentially, each
one building a Debian package for the targeted operating system. It is
designed to be a "single command" to produce all the images.
By default, builds for all known distributions, but a list of distributions
can be passed on the commandline for debugging.
Taken from https://github.com/matrix-org/synapse/blob/develop/debian/build_virtualenv, released under Apache 2

methods.name: sftkit.devel._debian.Builder.run_build
documentation: Build deb for a single distribution

classes.name: sftkit.http._context.ContextMiddleware
documentation:
FastAPI middleware to make any variable available in a request.
Works through ASGI magic: https://www.starlette.io/middleware/

# Usage:
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request, Depends
api = FastAPI()
context = Context(...)
api.add_middleware(ContextMiddleware,
                   context=context,
                   example_query="select version();")

# define dependency extractor
def get_context(request: Request) -> Any:
    return request.state.context

def get_db_pool(request: Request) -> asyncpg.Pool:
    return request.state.context.db_pool

async def get_db_conn(
    db_pool: asyncpg.Pool = Depends(get_db_pool),
) -> Connection:
async with db_pool.acquire() as conn:
    yield conn

# in the request:
@router.get("/dbversion")
async def dbver(req: Request,
                conn=Depends(get_db_conn),
                ctx=Depends(get_context)):
    query = req.state.args.example_query
    # another way:
    # example_value == ctx.example_value
    dbver = await conn.fetchrow(query)
    return {"db_version": f"{dbver[0]}"}

modules.name: sftkit.http._server
documentation: http server base class

functions.name: sftkit.http._server.use_route_names_as_operation_ids
documentation:
Simplify operation IDs so that generated API clients have simpler function
names.

Should be called only after all routes have been added.
